name: removed_container_owned_threads_leaked
code:
proof fn phi_2_removed_container_owned_threads_leaked(
    root_container: ContainerPtr,
    old_container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    new_container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    container_ptr: ContainerPtr,
    t: ThreadPtr,
)
    requires
        remove_container_ensures(root_container, old_container_perms, new_container_perms, container_ptr),
        old_container_perms[container_ptr].value().owned_threads@.contains(t),
    ensures
        !new_container_perms.dom().contains(container_ptr),
{
}
reason: remove_container_ensures removes the container from the domain but never requires its owned_threads to be empty or transferred — threads are silently leaked

name: parent_subtree_not_shrunk_after_remove
code:
proof fn phi_5_parent_subtree_not_shrunk_after_remove(
    root_container: ContainerPtr,
    old_container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    new_container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    container_ptr: ContainerPtr,
    ancestor: ContainerPtr,
)
    requires
        remove_container_ensures(root_container, old_container_perms, new_container_perms, container_ptr),
        old_container_perms[container_ptr].value().uppertree_seq@.contains(ancestor),
        new_container_perms.dom().contains(ancestor),
    ensures
        new_container_perms[ancestor].value().subtree_set@ =~= old_container_perms[ancestor].value().subtree_set@.remove(container_ptr),
{
}
reason: remove_container_ensures should shrink ancestor subtree_sets by removing the deleted container, but the spec may not enforce this for all ancestors
